title: Replication, Multi-Region Access, Backup, and Disaster Recovery · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs replication, multi-region access, backup, and disaster recovery from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for RPO, RTO, replication, failover, and restore dossier after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.
Configure versioning, destination ownership, IAM, KMS, filters, delete-marker behavior, replication status, metrics, and failure remediation.
Evaluate Multi-Region Access Points, latency routing, active-active writes, consistency, replication lag, failover controls, and client endpoint support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Durability Availability Recovery · changed case · Review Durability Availabi…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 6533 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate replication, independent backup, restore, and failover evidence; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.

Which action demonstrates Durability Availability Recovery without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Durability Availability Recovery · changed case · Review Durability Availabi….
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Durability Availability Recovery · changed case · Review Durability Availabi…, keep Durability Availability Recovery connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run controlled region, credential, deletion, corruption, dependency, and restore drills with abort, stakeholder communication, integrity checks, and lessons.

options.text: The Disaster Recovery Drill record says RPO, RTO, replication, failover, and restore dossier looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Disaster Recovery Drill.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient Disaster Recovery Drill happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Disaster Recovery Drill for RPO, RTO, replication, failover, and restore dossier.
The record names the assumption, applies Disaster Recovery Drill, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
